prose:
Frontier-orbital calculation on formaldehyde (CH₂O, 4 atoms: O, C, H, H) performed with DFT at the B3LYP/def2-TZVP level (tier 'standard', density-fit RI) using PySCF via chemkit. Gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1. Geometry taken from the user-supplied .xyz file; no geometry relaxation was performed (single-point on the input coordinates). SCF converged in 8 cycles. No warnings were reported (empty warnings array).

Headline result: HOMO–LUMO gap = 5.8696712 eV (Koopmans-style orbital gap).
HOMO (index 7) = −7.6523067 eV; LUMO (index 8) = −1.7826355 eV.
Total electronic energy = −114.55325 Hartree (−3117.1528 eV).

Koopmans descriptors: vertical IP ≈ 7.6523 eV, vertical EA ≈ 1.7826 eV, electronegativity = 4.7175 eV, chemical hardness = 2.9348 eV, electrophilicity index = 3.7914 eV.

Integrity: status 'ok', trustworthy = true; all checks passed (energy_finite, scf_converged, schema_header, schema_headline).

CLI: /home/erobles/chem-skills/mcp_server/chemkit_engine/cli.py frontier --method dft --functional b3lyp --basis def2-tzvp --tier standard [input.xyz]. Output: /home/erobles/chem-skills/.fidelity_scratch/tmpu5sp39gd/live.json.
